name: PR-Z 25: Circular Route of Herrera de los Navarros
name_original: Santuario de la Virgen de Herrera desde Herrerra de los Navarros
description: Hike the moderate PR-Z 25 Circular Route of Herrera de los Navarros, covering 9.9 miles with panoramic views and a historic sanctuary.
region_intro: Hiking around Herrera De Los Navarros explores a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, notably the Huerva River. The terrain offers a mix of open country and areas with varied vegetation. Elevation changes are present, providing diverse hiking experiences. The region's geology contributes to its distinct natural features. Best hiking trails around Herrera De Los Navarros The most popular hiking route is Hoces del Río Huerva from Vistabella, a 12.1 miles (19.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the Huerva River, offering varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is PR-Z 25: Circular Route of Herrera de los Navarros, a moderate 9.9 miles (16.0 km) path. This circular trail provides an extensive exploration of the local area. Local hikers also love the Ascent to the Herrera de los Navarros Hermitage (PR-Z 25), a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through the local hills, often completed in about 4 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Herrera De Los Navarros is defined by river paths, open countryside, and moderate ascents to local landmarks. The network offers options for both moderate and more challenging ability levels. The routes in Herrera De Los Navarros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from a few reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Herrera De Los Navarros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Herrera De Los Navarros?
A: There are 6 hiking routes available around Herrera De Los Navarros, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. These trails explore the local landscape, including river paths and open countryside.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in the area?
A: While there are no routes explicitly classified as 'easy' in our current selection, the region offers 5 moderate trails. These routes provide a good balance of challenge and accessibility for those with some hiking experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Regüelta el Trago – Hermitage of the Virgin of the Eagle loop from Paniza&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.4-mile (15.1 km) hike that offers varied terrain without extreme difficulty.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: Hiking around Herrera De Los Navarros features a landscape of rolling hills and river valleys, most notably the Huerva River. You'll encounter a mix of open country and areas with diverse vegetation, providing varied natural beauty. The region's geology also contributes to its distinct natural features.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976057277/pr-z-25-circular-route-of-herrera-de-los-navarros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-Z 25: Circular Route of Herrera de los Navarros&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.9-mile (16.0 km) path that offers an extensive exploration of the local area. Another circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Log Bridge of the Huerva River – Herrera de los Navarros loop from Herrera de los Navarros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the elevation changes like on the trails?
A: The trails around Herrera De Los Navarros feature moderate ascents and descents. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974604391/ascent-to-the-herrera-de-los-navarros-hermitage-pr-z-25" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Herrera de los Navarros Hermitage (PR-Z 25)&lt;/a&gt; involves an elevation gain of over 500 meters (1,640 feet) over its 9.1-mile (14.6 km) length, providing a good workout with rewarding views.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Several trails lead to or pass by notable landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Eagle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5199029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera de los Navarros&lt;/a&gt; itself is a point of interest, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4596548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moyuela watering trough&lt;/a&gt; offers a glimpse into local heritage.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Herrera De Los Navarros?
A: The trails in Herrera De Los Navarros are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.3 stars. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic river paths, and the opportunities for exploration within the local area.

Q: Is Herrera De Los Navarros suitable for family hikes?
A: The region offers moderate trails that can be suitable for families with some hiking experience. While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided, the moderate routes, with their varied terrain and points of interest, can be enjoyable for active families. Consider the distances and elevation gains when choosing a route for children.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Herrera De Los Navarros?
A: Many trails in the Herrera De Los Navarros region are generally dog-friendly, especially those in open countryside. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, particularly near livestock or in protected natural areas. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog, as water sources may be limited on some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Herrera De Los Navarros?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Herrera De Los Navarros. Temperatures are milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended if visiting during this period. Winter offers a different experience, but trails may be colder and potentially muddy.

Q: Are there good parking options near the trailheads?
A: Information on specific parking facilities for each trailhead is often available on individual route pages on komoot. Generally, in rural areas like Herrera De Los Navarros, you can find parking in or near the starting villages for the trails. It's recommended to check the route details before you go.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976052900/hoces-del-rio-huerva-from-vistabella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Río Huerva from Vistabella&lt;/a&gt; is classified as a difficult route. This 12.1-mile (19.4 km) trail follows the Huerva River and features varied terrain, taking approximately 5 hours and 10 minutes to complete.
tips: Beautiful and picturesque town in the interior of Teruel, very popular among hikers because it is the place chosen to start the ascent to the Sanctuary of the Virgen de Herrera.

The hermitage was erected 500 years ago in the place where, according to tradition, the Virgin appeared to Martín, a charcoal burner from the town who was in the mountains carrying out his work. The paved road ends at the sanctuary itself, so its traffic is limited.

faqs.question: How challenging is the PR-Z 25: Circular Route of Herrera de los Navarros?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered to be of moderate to challenging difficulty. It involves a significant elevation gain over its length, requiring a reasonable level of physical fitness. You'll encounter varied terrain, including forest tracks, narrow paths, and potentially some stony sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, traversing the Sierra de Herrera. You can expect to hike on forest tracks (pistas forestales), narrow paths (estrechos caminos y senderos), and potentially some stony or rocky areas (pedrizas y canchales) in the mountainous sections. Much of the route winds through beautiful pine forests.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the PR-Z 25 trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable features. It starts and finishes in Herrera de los Navarros, a charming village with historical heritage. A key destination is the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera, perched on Pico Herrera, offering extensive panoramic views. You might also encounter the 'Mirador de las Trincheras' (Trenches Viewpoint) and ancient Celtiberian settlements nearby.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The komoot data suggests an average duration of approximately 4 hours and 50 minutes for this 16 km hike. However, this can vary based on your fitness level, pace, and how much time you spend enjoying the viewpoints and highlights.

faqs.question: Where does the PR-Z 25: Circular Route of Herrera de los Navarros start, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts and finishes in the village of Herrera de los Navarros. Parking is generally available within the village, which serves as the main access point for the trail.

faqs.question: Is the PR-Z 25: Circular Route of Herrera de los Navarros dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where you might encounter livestock or wildlife, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are typically the best times to hike this route. The weather is usually milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential snow or colder conditions in winter. These seasons also offer beautiful natural scenery.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no specific permit required, nor are there any entrance fees to hike the PR-Z 25: Circular Route of Herrera de los Navarros. It is a publicly accessible hiking trail.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the PR-Z 25 is a well-established route. It is officially known as 'Ruta 13. PR-Z 25. Circular Herrera de los Navarros - Virgen de Herrera'. Sections of the trail also overlap with parts of the 'PR-Z 053' route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate to challenging difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate layers of clothing. A map or GPS device is also advisable, as well as a first-aid kit.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Herrera de los Navarros, you can find basic amenities, including cafes and potentially accommodation options, within the village itself. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially during off-peak seasons.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera
